title: Carbon and Its Compounds - Case Studies

questions.text: Identify the types of chemical reaction occurring during the combustion of fuel:
A student while walking on the road observed that a cloud of black smoke belched out from the exhaust stack of moving trucks. Choose the correct reason for the production of black smoke:
options: Limited supply of air leads to incomplete combustion of fuel.
Rich supply of air leads to complete combustion of fuel.
Rich supply of air leads to a combination reaction.
Limited supply of air leads to complete combustion of fuel.

questions.text: Although nitrogen is the most abundant gas in the atmosphere, it does not take part in combustion. Identify the correct reason for this statement.
options: Only hydrocarbons can take part in combustion
